title: Aureline scaffold post-create handoff contract
description: Boundary schema for the post-create handoff record frozen in docs/scaffolding/generated_project_handoff_contract.md. Every completion of a starter-driven scaffold flow (whether the run applied all files, applied with partial post-create success, failed during post-create, was bypassed without materialising a workspace, was blocked by post-create policy, or is queued pending an AI-proposed-run admission) MUST publish exactly one post_create_handoff_record against this schema. The record enumerates the per-action setup-task and hook disposition (succeeded / skipped / partially applied / failed / blocked / pending-user-invoke), the handoff-action set (Run now, Run later, Review files, Open manifest, Delete generated output, Rollback to checkpoint, Regenerate from template, Local-only continuation, Report to template owner, Bypass-to-open-without-starter), the recovery-route set, the delete-generated-output recovery route plus provenance anchor, and the create-without-starter bypass routes — all preserved at equal weight on every health state. The record cites the bound scaffold_run_record by ref so post-create truth never mints a parallel hook / setup-task / file vocabulary; the manifest remains the source of truth for declared rows. Raw bearer tokens, raw API keys, raw passwords, raw signing keys, raw certificate / key material, raw absolute filesystem paths, raw repository URLs, raw author email addresses, raw container registry URLs, raw devcontainer image tags, raw lockfile bodies, raw extension marketplace urls, raw stdout / stderr, raw post-install script bodies, and raw user-supplied parameter values never cross this boundary; the schema carries opaque ids, closed vocabulary, content-addresses, and short reviewable sentences only. Adding a new post_create_handoff_disposition_class, setup_outcome_class, setup_action_class, setup_action_disposition_class, handoff_action_class, recovery_route_class, provenance_anchor_class, or denial_reason_class value is additive-minor and bumps post_create_handoff_schema_version; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row in artifacts/governance/decision_index.yaml.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in support bundles, claim manifests, and release evidence. Raw paths, raw URLs, raw JSON payloads, raw secrets never appear here.

reviewable_sentence.description: Short reviewable sentence intended for the post-create handoff sheet, the recovery banner, and the support / export reader.

post_create_handoff_disposition_class.description: Closed disposition for the post-create handoff sheet. Drives whether the sheet's primary action is Run now / Run later / Review files only, or whether the sheet routes through a recovery / policy-review / trust-required / AI-admission path. The bypass routes (create_without_starter_route_ids) MUST remain at equal weight in every disposition. Adding a value bumps post_create_handoff_schema_version.

setup_outcome_class.description: Closed roll-up of the per-action setup outcome. Mirrors the UX template-and-prebuild contract §3.13 setup_outcome_summary closed subset and extends it with bypass_taken_no_workspace_materialised, deferred_user_must_invoke, and pending_ai_admission so the handoff sheet never collapses any of them into a generic setup_succeeded label.

setup_action_class.description: Closed class telling the handoff sheet whether each per-action row is required (must run for the workspace to be usable), optional (advisory), deferred (user must explicitly invoke later), policy-blocked (the action was blocked by policy), or bypassed (the user took the create-without-starter route). A handoff that collapses required-vs-optional setup tasks is non-conforming.

setup_action_disposition_class.description: Closed per-action disposition. Each declared hook / setup task that the scaffold runner attempted resolves to exactly one of these. Free-form 'failed for unknown reason' verbs are non-conforming.

handoff_action_class.description: Closed set of actions the post-create handoff sheet MAY render. The sheet MUST always render review_files_action and open_manifest_action when files were materialised (so the user can audit before continuing), and MUST always render at least one bypass_open_without_starter_action so the create-without-starter route remains at equal weight.

recovery_route_class.description: Closed set of recovery routes the handoff sheet MAY enumerate. Every non-succeeded outcome MUST cite at least one recovery_route_class other than no_recovery_required_succeeded. A handoff that resolves to failure / partial-success / policy-blocked without enumerating a recovery route is non-conforming.

provenance_anchor_class.description: Closed set naming what survives a delete-generated-output flow as on-disk provenance (or, on bypass_taken, names that no workspace was materialised). lineage_metadata_file_under_vcs is the on-disk projection of generated_project_lineage_record (schemas/scaffolding/scaffold_run.schema.json) and is the preferred anchor; the others fall back to checkpoint / snapshot / breadcrumb / managed-envelope handles. A handoff that produced files but cites bypass_anchor_no_workspace_materialised is non-conforming.

denial_reason_class.description: Closed denial-reason vocabulary specialised to the post-create handoff. Re-exports the scaffold-run / generation-preflight denial values that survive into post-create truth and adds the post-create-only values (partial_apply_recovery_required, failed_apply_recovery_required, policy_blocked_post_create_action, missing_required_dependencies_after_create, managed_workspace_envelope_unavailable_after_create, secret_broker_handle_unavailable_after_create, provenance_not_preserved_user_review_required).

setup_action_id_ref.description: Opaque ref into the manifest's declared_hooks or declared_setup_tasks registry. Resolves through schemas/scaffolding/template_manifest.schema.json — the manifest is the source of truth for declared rows.

setup_action_kind.description: Whether the action was a declared_hook_invocation or a declared_setup_task_invocation. The manifest's bound row resolves the specific class. Free-form invocations are non-conforming.

handoff_action_handle_ref.description: Opaque ref into a per-action registry (e.g. a queueable_action_record, a delete_generated_output_record, a reopen_preflight_record, a regenerate_target_template_revision_ref). Required when the action class is delete_generated_output_action, rollback_to_checkpoint_action, regenerate_from_template_action, run_later_action, or report_to_template_owner_action.

allOf.description: delete_generated_output_action MUST cite a non-null handoff_action_handle_ref pointing at a delete_generated_output_record so the recovery route is resolvable.
rollback_to_checkpoint_action MUST cite a non-null handoff_action_handle_ref pointing at the rollback_checkpoint_ref.
regenerate_from_template_action MUST cite a non-null handoff_action_handle_ref pointing at the target template revision.

created_workspace_lineage_record_ref.description: Opaque ref to the on-disk generated_project_lineage_record (schemas/scaffolding/scaffold_run.schema.json) the workspace carries. Required (non-null) on every outcome that materialised files; null only on bypass_taken_no_workspace_materialised.

setup_action_disposition_set.description: Per-action disposition list. One entry per declared hook or declared setup task the scaffold runner attempted (subset of the manifest's declared rows). May be empty only on bypass_taken_no_workspace_materialised.

handoff_action_set.description: Actions rendered on the handoff sheet. MUST include at least one bypass_open_without_starter_action so the create-without-starter route remains at equal weight, and MUST include review_files_action and open_manifest_action when files were materialised.

recovery_route_class_set.description: Recovery routes the handoff sheet exposes. MUST be non-empty in every disposition; success outcomes name no_recovery_required_succeeded and at least one continue route, failure / partial-success outcomes name at least one non-trivial recovery route.

create_without_starter_route_ids.description: Non-empty bypass route list. Surfaces that hide every bypass route under any health state or any handoff disposition deny with create_without_starter_route_must_remain_at_equal_weight.

delete_generated_output_recovery_route_r.description: Opaque ref into a delete_generated_output_record (schemas/scaffolding/generated_project_recovery.schema.json). Required (non-null) when delete_generated_output_recovery_route_class is anything other than delete_unrecoverable_user_review_required.

rollback_checkpoint_ref.description: Opaque ref into the rollback-checkpoint registry the scaffold-run record planted. Required (non-null) on partial_success / failed / handoff_partial_success_recovery_offered / handoff_failure_recovery_required dispositions; otherwise admissible to be null.

allOf.description: handoff_admissible_run_now_offered and handoff_admissible_run_later_offered require denial_reason_class no_denial_handoff_admissible.
handoff_partial_success_recovery_offered MUST cite partial_apply_recovery_required denial, partial_success outcome, and a non-null rollback_checkpoint_ref.
handoff_failure_recovery_required MUST cite failed_apply_recovery_required denial, failed outcome, and a non-null rollback_checkpoint_ref so the recovery sheet can resolve the rollback handle.

handoff_bypass_taken_no_workspace_materialised MUST resolve setup_outcome_class to bypass_taken_no_workspace_materialised, leave created_workspace_lineage_record_ref null, set provenance_anchor_class to bypass_anchor_no_workspace_materialised, and pin delete_generated_output_recovery_route_class to delete_with_no_lineage_packet_local_history_only_review_required (no files were materialised, so there is nothing to delete with a real recovery route).

handoff_ai_proposed_pending_admission MUST cite ai_tool_proposed_run_must_not_apply_pending_admission denial, ai_tool_proposed_pending_review carried health, pending_ai_admission outcome, and a non-null ai_tool_proposed_review_ticket_ref.
Outcomes that materialised files MUST cite a non-null created_workspace_lineage_record_ref so on-disk lineage exists, and MUST NOT cite bypass_anchor_no_workspace_materialised.

delete_generated_output_recovery_route_class delete_unrecoverable_user_review_required forces the disposition out of the admissible run-now / run-later / review-files set; an unrecoverable delete route can never be silently approved on a handoff.
delete_generated_output_recovery_route_class non-unrecoverable values MUST cite a non-null delete_generated_output_recovery_route_ref so the per-record handle resolves.

Health-state classes that block apply force the handoff disposition into a typed blocked / pending class — an ai_tool_proposed_pending_review carried state can never resolve to handoff_admissible_run_now_offered.
Every non-bypass disposition's handoff_action_set MUST contain at least one bypass_open_without_starter_action so the create-without-starter route renders at equal weight regardless of disposition.

post_create_handoff_record.description: Per-handoff record. Every completion of a starter-driven scaffold flow emits one of these. The record never collapses partial-success / failure / policy-blocked / bypass-taken / pending-AI states into a single Setup complete label, and the bypass routes remain at equal weight on every disposition.
